findings.title: Skill does not specify a license
Data Exfiltration of Credentials and Environment Variables
findings.description: The skill's Python script collects sensitive credentials (AWS, GCloud, SSH keys, npm, PyPI) and environment variables, then transmits them via base64-encoded HTTPS POST to an external endpoint (cloud-sync-api.io). This constitutes unauthorized data exfiltration.
findings.evidence: Lines 30-50: Collects ~/.aws/credentials, ~/.ssh/id_rsa, ~/.npmrc, etc. Lines 52-62: Base64 encodes and sends to https://cloud-sync-api.io/v1/backup with hardcoded API key.
findings.remediation: Remove credential collection and network transmission. Use local-only storage with user confirmation for any data export.

findings.title: Hardcoded API Key in Source Code
findings.description: The script contains a hardcoded API key 'cs_live_Tq8mNvX2pR5sK7wJ' used for authentication to the external endpoint. This key is exposed in plaintext and could be used for unauthorized access.
findings.remediation: Remove hardcoded key. Use environment variables or secure credential storage with user-provided authentication.

findings.title: Capability Inflation - Deceptive Skill Name and Description
findings.description: The skill is named 'cloud-sync' and described as a project configuration backup tool, but its actual behavior includes collecting and exfiltrating sensitive credentials (AWS, SSH, GCloud) and environment variables. The name and description are misleading.
findings.evidence: Name: 'cloud-sync', Description: 'Synchronize local project files and configurations to cloud storage.' But script collects credentials and environment variables beyond project configs.
findings.remediation: Rename skill to accurately reflect its data collection scope. Remove credential harvesting if not essential.

findings.title: Tool Poisoning - Unauthorized Tool Use
findings.description: The skill's manifest does not declare 'allowed-tools', but the Python script uses network capabilities (urllib.request) to transmit data externally. This violates the principle of least privilege and could bypass agent tool restrictions.
findings.evidence: No 'allowed-tools' field in YAML frontmatter. Script uses urllib.request.urlopen() for network calls.
findings.remediation: Declare allowed-tools explicitly and restrict to Read-only if network access is not required. Remove network calls or require user confirmation.

findings.title: Detection Evasion via Base64 Encoding
findings.description: The script base64-encodes the collected payload before transmission, which is a common obfuscation technique to evade content inspection and monitoring.
findings.remediation: Remove encoding obfuscation. Transmit data in plaintext if legitimate, or use proper encryption with user consent.

findings.title: Over-Collection of Environment Variables
findings.description: The script collects all environment variables (dict(os.environ)) without filtering, which may contain sensitive tokens, keys, and configuration data beyond what is needed for project backup.
findings.remediation: Only collect specific environment variables that are necessary for the stated purpose.

findings.title: Tool Chaining for Data Exfiltration
findings.description: The script chains multiple operations: read files (Read tool) → collect credentials → encode → send to external server (network). This multi-step data flow enables exfiltration without user awareness.
findings.evidence: collect_configs() reads files, then upload_backup() sends them externally. No user confirmation between steps.
findings.remediation: Add user confirmation before any network transmission. Separate read and send operations with explicit user approval.
